title: IG Markets Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)
meta_title: IG Markets Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026) | TradingToolsHub
meta_description: IG Markets Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). IG Markets (4.3★, Free). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using IG Markets — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
IG Markets is the world's largest CFD and spread betting provider, founded in 1974 and headquartered in London. With access to 17,000+ tradable markets across forex, stocks, crypto, futures, and options, plus professional ProRealTime charting included free, it's designed for traders who want institutional-grade tools without paying professional fees. The platform carries a 4.3/5 rating and offers free entry with no account minimums, making it accessible for beginners while powerful enough for advanced traders. IG Markets is best suited for CFD traders, spread bettors, and multi-asset traders who value regulatory oversight—the broker is regulated by the FCA, ASIC, and other top-tier authorities—over cutting-edge design.

What is IG Markets?

IG Markets is a London-based CFD and spread betting broker that has operated for over 50 years, serving more than 300,000 traders worldwide. The platform lets you trade CFDs (contracts for difference) on equities, indices, forex, cryptocurrencies, and commodities, or place spread bets if you're in the UK or Ireland. Unlike some brokers that charge subscription fees for advanced charting, IG includes ProRealTime—a professional-grade charting platform used by institutional traders—at no extra cost. The combination of low entry barriers (free to open an account), deep liquidity across 17,000+ markets, and regulatory credentials from the FCA and ASIC makes IG Markets the go-to choice for serious retail traders who don't want to compromise on security or market depth.

How to Create Your IG Markets Account

Opening an IG Markets account takes roughly 15–20 minutes if you have your documents ready. Here's the process:

  • Start the application: Go to IG Markets' website and click "Open an Account." Choose your account type: Standard (for most traders) or Professional (requires meeting specific criteria).
  • Enter personal information: Provide your full legal name, date of birth, email address, phone number, and residential address. IG uses this to verify your identity and comply with KYC (Know Your Customer) regulations.
  • Provide trading experience details: IG asks about your trading background, investment knowledge, and financial situation. These questions determine which leverage limits and product restrictions apply to your account. Honest answers prevent account suspensions later.
  • Identity verification: You'll need to upload a photo ID (passport or driver's license) and proof of address (utility bill or bank statement dated within 3 months). IG processes these automatically in most cases; verification typically completes within 1–2 hours.
  • Fund your account: IG accepts bank transfers, credit/debit cards, and e-wallets. There's no minimum deposit, so you can start with £10 or £100 to test the platform. Deposits appear instantly for cards and within 1–2 days for bank transfers.
  • Agree to terms: Review and accept IG's terms of service, privacy policy, and risk disclosures. Pay close attention to the inactivity fee clause: if your account sits untouched for 2 years, IG deducts $12/month until the account closes or you trade again.
  • Activate your account: Once verified, you'll receive login credentials via email. Log in and set your preferred two-factor authentication (SMS or authenticator app).

Setting Up IG Markets for the First Time

After logging in, spend 10 minutes configuring your workspace for efficient trading:

Dashboard layout: The IG web platform uses a customizable dashboard. On your first login, pin the "Markets" widget to your home view so you can quickly scan open trades and watchlists. Add the "News" widget below it to stay updated on market-moving events—IG aggregates newsfeed data directly into the platform.

Configure alerts: IG's alert system notifies you when markets hit price levels or economic events occur. Go to Settings → Alerts and set up price alerts on your key currency pairs or stock indices. For example, if you trade GBP/USD, set alerts at daily support and resistance. Economic alerts warn you before central bank announcements; enable these if you trade around data releases.

Link your broker (optional): If you're integrating IG with an external data feed or API, visit Settings → API Access. IG provides REST and WebSocket APIs for automated trading and real-time data. This step is only needed if you plan to build algo trading bots or pull live data into external software.

Customize ProRealTime: IG includes ProRealTime charting, which is separate from the web platform. Download the ProRealTime desktop app or use the browser version. Import custom indicators if you have them (ProRealTime supports ProBuilder scripting), or start with the 500+ built-in technical indicators. Save your layouts so your charts are ready when you log in each morning.

Enable risk management defaults: Under Settings → Risk Management, set your maximum daily loss limit and per-trade stop-loss requirements. IG enforces guaranteed stops on certain markets; enable these to protect against gap risk on low-liquidity assets.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. ProRealTime Charting

Most brokers charge $200–500/month for institutional charting software. IG includes ProRealTime free. Use it to backtest strategies, apply custom indicators, and generate automated trading signals. The backtesting tool lets you test your strategy on 20+ years of historical data before risking real capital—invaluable for validating trading systems.

2. Paper Trading

IG's practice account gives you virtual funds to trade live markets in real-time without risking money. Use paper trading to test new strategies, familiarize yourself with the platform, or practice trading at a specific time of day. Many traders paper-trade for 2–4 weeks before going live, building confidence and identifying gaps in their execution.

3. Mobile App

The IG mobile app (iOS/Android) mirrors the web platform's core functions: open trades, place orders, set alerts, and monitor watchlists. Markets move 24/5, so the mobile app lets you manage positions even when you're away from your desk. The app also includes live push notifications for price alerts and news events.

4. Automated Trading & API Access

IG's REST API lets developers build trading bots that execute strategies automatically. If you code in Python, C#, Java, or JavaScript, IG's API documentation is straightforward. Connect your bot to IG's live trading engine and let it execute signals without manual intervention. This is critical for traders who want to remove emotion from timing or scale across multiple instruments simultaneously.

5. Performance Analytics

IG's performance dashboard breaks down your trading by return, win rate, average trade duration, and profit factor. View metrics by instrument, time of day, or strategy type. Use these insights to identify your most profitable trading patterns and double down on what works while eliminating losers.

6. Risk Management Tools

Set stop-losses, take-profits, and guaranteed stops directly in your order ticket. IG's guaranteed stop-loss feature locks in your maximum loss on a trade, protecting you if a market gaps past your stop during low-liquidity hours (like around central bank announcements). The premium for guaranteed stops varies by asset but typically costs 0.5–2 pips above a standard stop.

7. IG Academy & Education

Leverage IG's educational resources: video tutorials, webinars, trading guides, and a free glossary covering everything from spreads to Greeks. IG's education team hosts weekly live sessions on technical analysis and risk management, directly accessible from the platform. New traders should spend 2–3 hours in the academy before their first live trade.

IG Markets Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

IG offers two account types, both free to open:

Standard Account ($0/month)

Designed for retail traders. Standard accounts cap leverage at 30:1 on major forex pairs, 20:1 on stocks, and 2:1 on crypto (following regulatory limits). Spreads vary by market but typically start at 0.6 pips on GBP/USD and 0.8 pips on EUR/USD. This is the account most traders should choose. There's no subscription fee, no minimum balance, and no platform charges—you only pay the spread when you trade and any guarantee premium if you use guaranteed stops.

Professional Account ($0/month, requires qualification)

Available to experienced traders who meet IG's criteria: typically £500,000+ in financial assets, 2+ years of professional experience, or employment in financial services. Professional accounts unlock leverage up to 500:1 on major forex pairs and tighter spreads due to exemptions from certain retail protections. If you don't qualify, IG won't force you; the Standard account is more appropriate for 95% of traders.

Important cost to know: If your account remains inactive (no trades) for 2 years, IG deducts $12/month until you trade, close the account, or deposit more funds. This is a real cost to monitor if you take extended trading breaks. Most active traders never encounter this fee.

Who should use IG Markets: Beginners get free access to institutional charting, education, and 17,000+ markets—a massive advantage over competitors like Interactive Brokers (which charges $10/month for thinkorswim on some tiers). Intermediate traders benefit from ProRealTime backtesting and API access. Advanced traders appreciate the breadth of instruments and low friction for swing trading and algorithmic strategies.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of IG Markets

1. Leverage ProRealTime's backtesting before trading live. Spend time in ProRealTime's Strategy Builder testing your setups on historical data. Set your parameters (entry rules, stop-loss, take-profit), run the backtest across multiple years, and review the statistics. A strategy with 55% win rate and 2:1 reward-to-risk ratio will typically profit long-term. Don't trade it live until backtesting confirms edge.

2. Use guaranteed stops on high-impact news events. Around major economic announcements (Non-Farm Payroll, ECB decisions, Fed statements), forex markets can gap 50–100 pips in seconds. If you're holding a position into these events, enable guaranteed stops. The 1–2 pip cost is cheap insurance against catastrophic slippage.

3. Set daily loss limits and honor them. IG's risk settings let you cap daily losses. If you hit the limit, the platform closes your positions and locks trading for 24 hours. This friction is intentional—it prevents revenge trading. Use it. Seriously.

4. Track performance by instrument and time of day. IG's analytics show which pairs or markets you trade best. If your data reveals you're profitable on EUR/GBP between 8am–12pm GMT but lose money on crypto at 2am, focus on your profitable window. Specialization beats broad mediocrity.

5. Test strategies on the paper trading account first. Never trade a new setup with real money on day one. Paper trade it for 20–50 cycles so you're confident in execution and position sizing. This separates traders with systems from traders playing with luck.

6. Use IG's mobile app only for monitoring and emergency closes. The app is good for checking open trades and cutting losses mid-day. Don't initiate new positions on mobile—you'll make rushed decisions without your full chart setup and risk framework. Mobile is defensive, not offensive.

7. Monitor the economic calendar built into IG. High-impact events are color-coded red and flagged in your news feed. Before entering any position, check the calendar for the next 24 hours. A surprise central bank statement can wipe out a small account; knowing it's coming lets you adjust position size or step aside.

Common IG Markets Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue 1: "My order won't fill during volatile markets."
Solution: IG widens spreads and occasionally requotes orders during high volatility (major economic events, earnings announcements). If you see a requote, you have 1–2 seconds to accept the new price or cancel. To avoid this: enter orders during calmer market hours, use limit orders instead of market orders (they guarantee your entry price but may not fill instantly), or accept the wider spread during volatile periods as a cost of trading.

Issue 2: "I'm losing money on spreads before the trade even moves."
Solution: Spreads on IG are competitive but not the tightest in the industry. On major pairs like EUR/USD, expect 0.8–1.2 pips. If you're day trading and exiting after a 5-pip move, the spread eats 16–24% of your profit. Switch to longer timeframes (swing trading, not day trading) or trade only the most liquid instruments (major forex pairs, indices, tech stocks). Comparing IG to Saxo Bank or OANDA might reveal tighter spreads on your preferred markets.

Issue 3: "The mobile app crashed and I can't close a losing trade."
Solution: Download the web app backup (accessible from any browser) so you're not dependent on a single client. Bookmark IG's web platform on your phone's home screen. Keep IG's customer support number (+44 20 7111 1234 for UK clients) saved in your phone. If the app crashes, call support—they can close positions manually in seconds while you regain access.

Issue 4: "I got a margin call and my position was closed."
Solution: IG enforces margin requirements: maintain at least 50% of your required margin or face automatic position closures. To prevent this, reduce position size, set trailing stops so you exit winning trades and lock in gains, or keep 30%+ of your account balance in cash as a buffer. Use IG's "Available to Borrow" metric in Settings to understand your real leverage capacity at all times.

Is IG Markets Worth It? Our Verdict

IG Markets is worth it if you value regulatory oversight, educational resources, and professional tools at zero subscription cost. The 4.3/5 rating reflects consistent execution, not cutting-edge UI design. For CFD traders and spread bettors who want ProRealTime charting, backtesting, 17,000+ tradable markets, and FCA regulation without monthly fees, IG is hard to beat—it's our top pick for traders who refuse to compromise on broker credibility. The inactivity fee and regional restrictions (no CFDs for US clients, spread betting UK/Ireland only) are minor drawbacks for the intended audience. Compare IG Markets to Interactive Brokers or Saxo Bank if you're deciding between institutional-grade platforms; IG wins on free charting and ease of use, while those competitors may offer tighter spreads on specific instruments. For most traders 2026, IG Markets is a reliable, fully-featured home base.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
